{"society_power": 2.5, "state_power": 5.0, "society_change": -1.5, "state_change": 3.5, "key_event": "Reza Khan's 1921 coup and Pahlavi state centralization", "reasoning": "Reza Khan built a unified army, crushed provincial revolts, rationalized fiscal administration, and founded the Pahlavi dynasty, transforming a collapsed state (1.5) into a functioning centralizing autocracy with real bounded reach (5.0). Simultaneously, disarmament of tribes, marginalization of the ulama and old aristocracy, and coercive cultural decrees eroded autonomous societal power centers, dropping society toward weak/sporadic mobilization."}
